workers.summary: protocol.json defines global required_fields incl. evidence, but multiple ledger entries omit evidence; enforcement appears advisory (prompt-only), not validated by launcher.

Protocol mandates malformed-trailing-line tolerance in text only. The sole concrete consumer (stampede-monitor.sh) uses wc -l, which is incidentally tolerant but performs no JSON parsing. No line-by-line parse-and-skip logic exists anywhere. AI commanders receive the ledger paths via env vars and are expected to self-implement tolerance per prompt guidance, not hardcoded logic.

Absent collab ledgers are handled gracefully by the monitor and explicitly required by protocol; launcher pre-creates protocol + ledger files in metaswarm runs, so absence is unlikely at start but still possible later (e.g., deletion/race).

Claimed cleanup is sequentially linked to result creation in all documented workflows, with atomic write-before-remove ordering. Recovery logic exists but has gaps in commander-aware detection and timing window protection.
source_refs are required by protocol/launcher and appear in commander-001’s bundle, but commander bundle schema omits adopted_consensus/source_refs and validation is not enforced.

synthesis.overall_assessment: Collaboration-bus QA found one hard contract failure and four material gaps: required_fields are not enforced uniformly across live ledgers, malformed-trailing-line tolerance is advisory rather than implemented, absent-ledger handling is graceful mainly in the monitor, claimed cleanup/result linkage is ordered but non-transactional, and adopted source_refs are required but not validated end-to-end.
blockers: reviews.jsonl entries omit protocol-required evidence, and at least one consensus plus one broadcast entry also omit evidence
No implementation performs JSONL parse-and-skip for malformed trailing lines despite protocol guidance
majors: Absent ledgers degrade to zero counts in the monitor, but runtime consumers rely on startup creation and prompt discipline rather than hard guarantees
Claimed cleanup follows atomic result writes, but crash windows and inconsistent orphan-recovery thresholds can still produce duplicate-work risk
Launcher prompt and protocol require source_refs/adopted consensus traceability, but commander bundle schema docs and runtime validation lag behind
synthesis.traceability_verdict: Partial: protocol/source_refs intent is strong, but live validation and adoption-proof enforcement remain incomplete.
